pi_items.source: Image downloaded successfully from https://imagej.net/images/AuPbSn40.jpg. Public, non-synthetic data. No indication in metadata or filename that it is synthetic.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real AuPbSn40.jpg image (600×412 pixels) from the ImageJ repository, representing an eutectic microstructure. The image is calibrated at 0.49 µm/pixel (asserted but not embedded in image metadata). FFT analysis on the downloaded image produces a ground truth lamellar spacing of 42.0000 µm at frequency peak radius 7 pixels. This spacing value is significantly larger than typical eutectic lamellar spacings (0.1-3 µm reported in literature), suggesting the FFT captures large-scale microstructural features rather than individual lamellar spacing at this magnification. The task expects output files (FFT spectrum, bandpass filtered image, line profile CSV, report text), with measurement accuracy tolerances of ±40%. All file paths and directory structures are correctly specified.
